summary: Common Blue Pill-compatible STM32F103C8T6 development board.

capabilities.summary: PC13 is commonly wired to the on-board status LED on Blue Pill-compatible boards.
notes: Many Blue Pill variants drive the PC13 LED as active-low.

capabilities.summary: Many Blue Pill variants route PA11 and PA12 to the board USB connector, which makes USB FS device projects practical when the clone follows the common reference layout.
notes: Verify the exact clone wiring before relying on USB device mode.

capabilities.summary: Prefer CAN1 remap on PB8 and PB9 to keep PA11 and PA12 free for USB FS on mixed-interface projects.
notes: External CAN transceiver hardware is still required.

clock_profile.summary: Common Blue Pill clock tree using an 8 MHz HSE and PLL x9 for a 72 MHz system clock.
notes: Blue Pill variants commonly ship with an 8 MHz external crystal and run at 72 MHz via PLL x9.
Blue Pill-compatible boards commonly expose PA11 and PA12 to the USB connector, but the exact wiring depends on the vendor variant.
Many variants provide an on-board LED on PC13, often active-low.
sources: Common STM32F103C8 Blue Pill board layout conventions
